body: Honestly, the USA dragging teams into playing CONCACAF soccer when they have the lead is a pretty damn good idea
Aussies desperate to have the yanks as their rival

You think the Americans wanted to do that?

The Australian fans were priding themselves on physical play and shithousery.   They treat is as a core strategy of theirs.

And ironically the Australians in the A-League sub seriously thought that Americans were soft *because* they play in CONCACAF.  They clearly aren't familiar with the region.

e: The Australians thought they could have their way playing rough and the Americans showed them they have decades of experience playing this type of ball when necessary.

I know a lot of shit is flying in this thread between countries; clearly the better team won IMO, huge mistake not to to start Irankunda and Metcalfe, although I do think the ref was punishing us more for shithousery which both teams were willful participants in, but shout out the city of Seattle, man. Was supposed to fly back (to NYC) tomorrow, but during extra time I ambitiously booked a last minute flight set to depart in an hour and a half during extra time and unbelievably I got to the gate with time plenty of time to spare. 

Even just the thoughts of attempting this in Melbourne from the MCG or in NYC from MSG/Yankee Stadium/Citifield/MetLife after a game would leave you traumatised for life.

Edit: No shade meant against the city of Seattle btw, love Seattle and the whole PNW (the 101 stretch from NorCal to WA is my fav part of the US), I'd love to stay longer, just realised I could be back in NYC by midnight and have something there to be back for so took the flight.

I know people are saying it was a more competitive second half - sure, but we were in game management mode after 60 mins and at no point did I see a single shot from the Aussies that looked super dangerous tbh. So all in all I think we won convincingly
Someone's having a rough day
Ah you think shithousery is your ally? You merely adopted the shithouse. We were born in it, molded by it.

Concacaf is the name of the regional football federation that the United States plays in for qualification to the World Cup and tournaments like the Gold Cup. It includes teams like Mexico, Canada, Panama, Costa Rica, Jamaica, and Honduras just to name a few. The games played between these countries are notoriously chippy, lots of fouls and generally rough and less polished play. The poster was implying that we tapped into those dark arts in the second half of the game especially to seal the victory.

> we just hate your country, culture, and people 

Speak for yourself, cunt.  A lot of us love yanks but, like Australia, we both have our share of fuckwits.

From what I have been seeing on social media, the world has been loving the US. Like the person said below, go find yourself a hug

BTW nobody should want to see any country fail in any capacity other maybe sports. How sick are you? All countries have great people... including yours... minus you.
We have Canada and Mexico for rivals. No Aussies needed.
Touch grass, this is such a chud ass take.

Got to watch the full match after recording it as I couldn't watch it in the afternoon.

It was going to be impossible for the US to match the performance against Paraguay but I don't know how you can come away from today without being impressed. That was a damn solid 1sr half. No Pulisic. McKennie was brilliant. Tillman did one hell of a job creating the free kick with his physicality that led to Freeman's goal. Dest had sequences where he created with some magic.

The 2nd half wasn't anything to write home about. Australia played with good physicality and their best chance was probably the corner where Richards went down. Balogun getting chased down on his chance in the 51st minute or so was probably the best of the bunch for the US. The reality is, you look at other matches where there's an even greater chasm of talent and Brazil "only" beat Haiti, 3-0. 

With Turkey's loss to Paraguay, winning Group D with only two matches played is unbelievably impressive.

Congrats USA! I didnt watch the first game so didnt know what to expect. Your boys are fast and athletic with good positioning.

Australia I love you but midfield needs sorting imo. I feel that no one can control the ball/game.  The panic was definitely felt after the first goal but even then, there is still so much time to level. 

On to Paraguay. Hope Australia wins 💪

Incredibly frustrating game. We were definitely the worse team and deserved to lose but felt like we handicapped ourselves for no reason by not playing any of our biggest threats on the counter. 

We had no way to release pressure in the first half, and when we put on the team that should’ve started, the game was already gone.

Hats off to the US, they played very well, but I can’t help but feel frustrated that we never really got to see our best lineup play at parity.
Stay mad
The offside goal? Dude he was clearly onside. And both sides were throwing their bodies around a litttttttttle too much. Both parties got away with fouls early and got called for the same ones late.
We call our team the Socceroos
DOS A CERO, MATE!

We’re a solid quarterfinal team. No better no worse. Considering USMNT history I’m cool with that
No one out shithouseries concacaf. I mean this is normal to us, including the shit quality of the refs. Basically.a concacaf game from start to finish..

Dest, Tillman, and Robinson were unplayable in the first half, saying both teams had one shot on target is a gross misrepresentation. The own goal doesn't count as a shot on target but it was a high xg chance if balo puts a boot on it. 

You deserved a goal in the second half but the overall result is fair.

I agree that Aus made the USA look average in the second half. Both teams had moments.

It just Reddit

Start by saying we (Aus) did not deserve to win.

I will say though, I do not think the US played as well as people are saying. Do keep in mind, both teams went into half time with 1 shot on target each. Though US had a lot of the ball, they really weren't creating much, which was the same when we played Turkiye.

People can over-inflate one team looking better if the other is set-up more defensively like that.

I am baffled that we didn't get a penalty for the handball that occurred when a defender was pushing a jumping Irankunda, but I dont see the accidental trip on Metcalfe as a penalty so not mad at that.

Just overall disappointing first half with a better second half, still not sure why Metcalfe and Irankunda didn't start.
Go outside dude. Find something to enjoy that isn’t hate.
It’s weird because anything beyond the R16 seems like it would take a miracle, but anything less would feel like a monumental failure.

I'm Canadian, I went in saying it's a failure if we don't get out of the group, expectations met if we get to the R32, exceeded if R16.

Realistic expectations always help.
Ream is gonna be a liability against a top level squad that can press.

IFAB rules clarifying natural positioning, proximity and ball-to-hand will explain the non-call. Pretty cut and dry when it was waved away INSTANTLY on field and also checked by var. 

Low shots on goal but AUS xG of .44 to U.S.A. 1.3 which shows the USA was more efficient, certainly not dominant by any means even with 70% possession in the first half which is what a lot of people will point to as “dominant”. 

Odd team selection for sure.

Hopefully we go through after playing Paraguay and Popovic actually gives his team a chance.
I love outback
If he doesn’t OG we are scoring it anyways. He had to try to play the ball.

There is no natural body movement that justifies the arms being outstretched away from the body like that. Pushing an opponent is not a justifiable natural movement.

"By having their arm in such a position, the player takes the risk of their arm being hit by the ball and being penalised".

One can recognise that this was a clear as day handball without needing to believe that the result of the match would have been different if it was given. I can't comprehend the reaching people are doing to justify it. If he jumps for the ball and the arms go out to the side a bit, that's natural. What we saw was unequivocally not a natural position as he did not need to be pushing his opponent to contest that ball.

"touches the ball with their hand/arm when it is in a position that makes their body unnaturally bigger and that position is not the result of their body moving fairly as part of play"

I mean my argument is the way he is pushing the player is not their body moving fairly as part of play, there is a solid amount of outstretch there.
Lmao
These guys don’t know about playing in Kingston 🇯🇲 on a random night in October. 🔪🩸💣⚽️🍻
Aussies would would love playing in concacaf.  So much bullshit all the time, but once you embrace the mud, you crave it

We have four different sports that could be 'football' over here lmao depending on where and who you're talking to.

The US look like the best of the rest (sorta) but miles off the best in the world. They looked pretty bad in the second half and their defense is highly questionable. Very curious what happens when they come up against a team with attacking intent.

I do love that their first thought is to be brave and attack, so many teams in these tournaments just sit back and it's frustrating - and that's exactly what Australia did. They sort of figured it out by the end but it was too late.

I know America is 'murica and people love to meme the whole "wtf is a kilometre" thing, I get it, and have fun. But some takes in here are fucking unbelievable. Give your head a shake. 

Can see them easily getting to the Round of 16 and then being hastily eliminated, but if the draw is kind, R8 is possible. I do think their elimination will be explosive though.
Agreed. Turkey will be an interesting test, but as soon as we come up against a real team (Germany, England, France, etc.) we are in trouble. Of course, weird shit happens all the time..

>but I dont see the accidental trip on Metcalfe as a penalty so not mad at that.

For me that was clear penalty. 

The Aussie player was in front of the US player, who steps on his achilles

Doesn't really matter if it was accidental, it's still a clear foul in the box

You can compare it with the penalty against Modric where his foul was also accidental

My experience is similar. Never met an Aussie in the states who wasn’t a good time. That’s why I’m surprised with all this one sided beef from their media. So far looks to be a small minority of morons whose voices on social media are louder than most Americans in real life

Our midfield has historically been a weakness (we struggle to keep the ball against many teams in Asia that can't even qualify) and to be fair the Turkey result was a big surprise to me. The only thing I can think of was that Turkey were happy to let us try and build from the back after losing possession which hid our deficiencies somewhat, but USA rarely let us keep the ball with their aggressive press.  

There's a reason why we play a low block and go for counters; we can't play through the midfield unless the other team lets us

I saw a second half where we gassed out at 60 min,, Australia attempted to drag the US into a fistfight only bordering soccer, and we shithoused as good as we got and beat you at your own game.

I'm not surprised at all by your inability to follow a topic through just three comments.

The point made was that the US did not play that well. You were disputing that, so I went out of my way to do my best to explain it to you. I realise now that that was a waste of time.

They played well enough to win against a poor opponent.

They did not say that, so that's probably where your misunderstanding is coming from.

They used it as a metric to say the US did not play as well as people think.

I agreed with you that that metric did not reflect the first half, so explained further reasons behind the argument "the US did not play as well as people think". Because that is a true statement.

It was nonetheless a good win.

To sort of explain it a bit better I'll say that Australia were not at it in the first half. If Australia had played the way they did against Turkiye, the USA would not have threatened them with the chances created, being an own goal where the defender panicked into a poor decision, and a fortunate deflection amidst a defensive lapse.

That, I think, is the idea. But that's not to say the USA didn't have further levels to go to had they not scored the two soft goals. It's just a simple fact that the USA did not create that many threatening chances in the match. Definitely played better though.

Australia will feel that the result was relatively self-inflicted considering the nature of the two goals and overall play.

Mmm I see. I've only ever heard it used in regards to describing politics. You do seem like an asshole though for someone asking a question.

I guess the question is whether using your hands to foul an opponent in the box is a "natural position". It certainly wasn't a situation where you would say it was proximity or ball-to-hand. That would be where the defender had no chance to move their hand. 

I think if this had been given as a pen in a non host nation match, it wouldn't have been particularly controversial. 

That said, I think had some of these odd calls gone against the US, they would've been able to keep the foot on the gas for longer and still won pretty comfortably.

Atrocious second half from the ref. Blatant handball in the box, ankle stomp in the box, and a two handed shove on Irankunda as he was going for a header in the box, yet no penalties given. I don't think it would have been enough to change the result, but the score ending 2-0 instead of 2-1 could make a difference going into the final matchday if Paraguay beat Turkiye.

It's not even remotely "unbelievably impressive". Winning vs Paraguay and Australia should be expected, and the fact that Turkiye shat the bed doesn't add to your impressiveness. 

So all in all, the US winning group D with only two matches is because you had one of the easiest groups where the only real threat beforehand did fuck all.

I have no idea why Irankunda and Metcalfe weren't put on at the start. We were playing poorly in the first half and it cost us dearly. Could've turned it around and that was clear in the 2nd half. But that reffing was horrific. Clearly so one-sided with so many clear fouls like even shirt pulling and loads of others, dirty play from USA blatantly ignored. Whereas whenever that number 4 started crying over the wind changing he got the ref's full attention. Calling a hydration break _during_ one of our corner kicks?? Offside goal just took it too far. You can be subtly biased, and then you can be whatever the fuck that was. Afraid of being deported I guess! Though he's been banned for match fixing before:

We don't see america as a rival in any sporting sense. Our rivals in football are Japan, Iran, and Uruguay. Maybe South Korea and Saudi Arabia too.

When it comes to america we just hate your country, culture, and people for reasons completely unconnected to football. When we say we desperately want to beat america, it isn't "we need to beat our sporting rivals", it's "we want to see america fail in every capacity (including but not limited to sport) because it's what they as a country deserve".
